pr.title: Cleanup incbins in data, work on pointer cleaning
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly/data cleanup PR focused on replacing raw incbin-style or opaque data words with explicit labels, NULL pointer tokens, function pointers, strings, and section splits. The main impact was in asm/data.s, with additional pointer/data cleanup spread across fighter, item, menu, lb, ef, text, extab, extabindex, bss, and sysdolphin baselib assembly files. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice, so intent is inferred primarily from the title and diff.
what_changed: Reworked asm/data.s at very large scale, with 108926 added lines and 12580 deleted lines in the provided changed-file summary.
Replaced many raw zero word entries such as `.4byte 0x00000000` with `.4byte NULL`, making probable null pointer slots explicit in data tables.

Moved or removed the lbcommands table from the top of asm/data.s in the excerpt, including entries for Command_00, Command_04, Command_08, func_800059DC, func_80005A30, func_80005A88, func_80005AC4, func_80005AE4, func_80005B00, and func_80005B18.
Split bss skip ranges into more granular labeled symbols, for example splitting lbl_8045996C and adding lbl_804599F0, and splitting lbl_80459A8C with added lbl_80459A98 and lbl_80459B28.

Added pointer/data cleanup hunks across many assembly files under asm/melee, including ft/code_8006DBF4.s, ef/code_8005B4B8.s, ftkirby.s, ftmario.s, gm/code_801601C4.s, item files, text_2.s, and text_4.s.
Expanded exception metadata assembly in asm/extab.s and asm/extabindex.s, suggesting additional explicit exception table/index data replaced opaque bytes or unresolved data.
Added small baselib pointer/data cleanups in class.s, dobj.s, fog.s, jobj.s, lobj.s, mobj.s, pobj.s, and wobj.s.
smart_moves: Used `NULL` instead of raw `0x00000000` for word-sized zero entries where the data appears to be pointer-like, improving readability and making future struct recovery easier.

Introduced intermediate bss labels rather than leaving large anonymous `.skip` regions, which helps future references land on named storage and can reveal separate globals inside a formerly monolithic block.
Kept cleanup at the assembly/data level rather than pretending recovered C types were known; this is appropriate when evidence supports pointer slots but not complete struct declarations.
Preserved function pointer table entries as symbol references, which helps identify dispatch tables and callback arrays without needing full decompilation of the owning module.
Applied pointer cleaning broadly across modules, reducing the amount of opaque data future contributors must inspect by hand.
decomp_lessons: When reducing incbins or opaque data, converting obvious pointer-zero words to `NULL` is a low-risk readability improvement, but only when the context supports pointer interpretation.
Large `.skip` bss regions should be split when addresses inside them are known or referenced; named bss subranges are valuable stepping stones toward real globals.
Function pointer arrays should be emitted with function symbols instead of raw addresses or anonymous words whenever targets are known.
Data cleanup can be useful even before C decompilation: symbolic tables, strings, NULLs, and labeled storage make later struct inference and matching work easier.
Extab/extabindex data should be made explicit when possible because exception metadata can otherwise hide symbol boundaries and complicate section matching.
For massive data.s cleanups, future agents should rely on concrete diff patterns and changed-file summaries rather than overclaiming full semantic recovery.
naming_conventions: Unidentified data and bss symbols continue to use address-derived `lbl_XXXXXXXX` names, such as lbl_803B9928, lbl_803BA248, lbl_804599F0, lbl_80459A98, and lbl_80459B28.
Known or partially known functions use `func_XXXXXXXX` names, as seen in function pointer tables with func_800059DC, func_80013BB0, func_80014014, and func_80014234.
The PR used the assembler-level `NULL` token for null pointer words instead of retaining raw `.4byte 0x00000000` in pointer-like contexts.
Module/file organization follows the existing asm tree: asm/melee subsystem folders such as ft, ef, lb, gm, it, mn, and asm/sysdolphin/baselib component files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace raw zero pointer words with `.4byte NULL` to document pointer table slots while preserving exact emitted data.
Replace anonymous or incbin-derived words with explicit `.4byte` symbol references for function pointers and data labels.
Split `.skip` directives in bss to create labels at meaningful internal offsets without changing total section size.
Use `.asciz` and `.balign 4` where string boundaries are known, preserving alignment while making embedded strings searchable.
Keep address-derived labels when semantics are unknown; this avoids inventing names while still improving relocation and cross-reference clarity.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace remaining `.4byte 0x00000000` entries in asm/data.s with `NULL` or typed symbols.
Search for uses of lbl_804599F0, lbl_80459A98, and lbl_80459B28 to determine whether these bss splits later became named globals.
Inspect later lbcommand or lbcommands PRs to see where the removed table from asm/data.s was relocated or typed.
Search for subsequent conversions of baselib class/dobj/jobj/lobj/mobj/pobj/wobj assembly data into headers or C structs.
Investigate whether extab/extabindex explicit data from this PR enabled later section matching or exception table cleanup.
